verdict.summary: Guide is broadly useful, but it has several misleading statements on AML/deposit thresholds, non-resident cash limits, foreign/Russian cards and fee presentation.

issues.current: Внесение наличных свыше 10 000 € требует обоснования происхождения средств по правилам AML
issues.problem: This states a hard €10,000 cash-deposit source-of-funds rule. In Portuguese AML rules the relevant due-diligence threshold for occasional transactions is generally €15,000, while €10,000 is mainly the EU customs cash declaration threshold for entering/leaving the EU. Banks can request source-of-funds documents at any amount on a risk basis, but there is no simple automatic 'cash deposit over €10,000 requires proof' rule.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: "Банк может запросить подтверждение происхождения средств при любой сумме по риск-профилю клиента; для разовых операций около/выше 15 000 € due diligence особенно вероятна. Порог 10 000 € относится прежде всего к таможенному декларированию наличных при въезде/выезде из ЕС, а не к универсальному лимиту внесения на счёт."

issues.current: ✈️ Нерезиденты-туристы (физлица без обязанности вести организованную бухгалтерию в Португалии): порог обычно **≥ 10 000 €** для операций с наличными без специальных процедур AML
issues.problem: This conflates the LGT cash-payment limit for non-residents with AML/customs procedures. The €10,000 figure in art. 63-E LGT is a cash payment prohibition threshold for non-resident individuals not acting as entrepreneurs/traders; it is not a general threshold for 'cash operations without special AML procedures'.
issues.suggestion: Write instead: "✈️ Физлица-нерезиденты в Португалии, если они не действуют как предприниматели/коммерсанты: наличный платёж запрещён при сумме **≥ 10 000 €**. Это отдельное правило LGT; оно не отменяет AML-проверки банка и таможенное декларирование наличных при пересечении границы ЕС."

issues.problem: Misleading as a cost line: Multibanco usually does not add a local ATM surcharge, but withdrawal with a foreign card is often not actually free because the card issuer may charge withdrawal fees, FX markup, cash-advance fees for credit cards, or apply a bad exchange rate if DCC is accepted.
issues.suggestion: Change amount to variable/unknown instead of 0, e.g. "amountEUR": null, "note": "Сбор Multibanco обычно 0 €, но итоговая стоимость зависит от банка-эмитента, типа карты, FX и DCC; независимые ATM могут брать отдельный сбор."
issues.source: Banco de Portugal guidance on ATM fees; card issuer fee schedules

issues.current: Обычно принимают большинство карт Visa/Mastercard/Maestro ... принятие конкретной иностранной карты зависит от эмитента, сети, санкционных ограничений
issues.problem: For the Russian-speaking target audience this is too soft: Russian-issued Visa/Mastercard cards generally do not work in Portugal/abroad since the 2022 suspension of international operations by Visa and Mastercard for Russian banks; Mir is not accepted in Portugal. Saying only that acceptance depends on issuer/sanctions may mislead Russian newcomers.
issues.suggestion: Add explicit caveat: "Важно для карт РФ: Visa/Mastercard, выпущенные российскими банками, как правило не работают в Португалии; карты Мир в Multibanco не принимаются. Нужна карта банка вне РФ или португальский/европейский счёт."
issues.source: Visa and Mastercard public suspension notices, March 2022

issues.current: 💰 Снятие наличных — 0 € для карт своего банка
issues.problem: Needs a card-type caveat. Debit-card cash withdrawals are normally free in Multibanco, but credit-card cash advances may incur issuer fees/interest even at the cardholder's own bank.
issues.suggestion: Write: "💰 Снятие наличных дебетовой картой своего банка — обычно 0 €; по кредитной карте возможны комиссии/проценты за cash advance по тарифам эмитента."
issues.source: Banco de Portugal / bank fee schedules

issues.current: ⚖️ Штрафы по RGIT за нарушение — могут быть значительными; контроль осуществляет AT
issues.problem: The guide gives a fine range later, but here does not distinguish individuals and companies. The €180–€4,500 range is the commonly cited range for individuals; for collective persons/legal entities the practical maximum can be higher under RGIT rules.
issues.suggestion: Add: "Для физлиц обычно указывают 180–4 500 €; для юрлиц/коллективных субъектов диапазон может быть выше по правилам RGIT."
issues.source: RGIT, art. 129 and general rules on coimas

issues.problem: Presented as a universal fine range. It should specify that this is the typical individual-person range; legal entities may face higher amounts.
issues.suggestion: Change label/note to: "Штраф для физлица за нарушение лимита наличных — 180–4 500 €; для юрлиц диапазон может быть выше."

issues.current: банк обязан подать suspicious transaction report в UIF/Polícia Judiciária
issues.problem: In Portugal suspicious transaction reporting is generally communicated to DCIAP and UIF. UIF is part of Polícia Judiciária, but naming only UIF/PJ is incomplete for a legal/AML explanation.
issues.suggestion: Write: "банк обязан направить comunicação de operação suspeita в DCIAP и UIF/PJ по Lei 83/2017".

issues.current: "Banco de Portugal — troca de notas e moedas danificadas", "url": "https://www.bportugal.pt/"
issues.problem: The source URL is only the Banco de Portugal homepage, not the actual page on damaged banknotes/coins. This is weak for a practical instruction.
issues.suggestion: Replace with the specific Banco de Portugal page for damaged euro banknotes/coins exchange, or at least a direct search/result URL under bportugal.pt for "troca de notas danificadas".

issues.current: Sources include LGT, Lei 92/2017, generic Banco de Portugal homepage, generic SIBS homepage.
issues.problem: Several sensitive claims lack direct sources: AML due diligence thresholds, EU cash declaration, Russia/Belarus deposit sanctions, and RGIT fine range.
issues.suggestion: Add sources: Lei n.º 83/2017 for AML; Regulation (EU) 2018/1672 for cash entering/leaving the EU; Regulation (EU) 833/2014 art. 5b and Regulation (EC) 765/2006 for sanctions; RGIT art. 129 for fines.
issues.source: Lei n.º 83/2017; Regulation (EU) 2018/1672; Regulation (EU) 833/2014; Regulation (EC) 765/2006; RGIT

missing_topics: Explicit note that Russian-issued Visa/Mastercard generally do not work in Portugal and Mir is not accepted
Clear distinction between cash-payment limits under LGT, AML bank checks, and EU customs cash declaration rules
Specific sources for AML, sanctions, EU cash declaration and RGIT fines
Caveat that credit-card ATM withdrawals may be treated as cash advances with fees/interest
